description: Golden dataset for prompt analysis regression tests. Calibrated to current parser/scorer behavior. Tiered suites: core (fast) vs full (complete). Run scripts/calibrate-validation-prompts.ts to regenerate after intentional changes.

componentLegend.role: Role Definition - Explicit role or persona assigned to the AI
componentLegend.context: Context - Background information relevant to the task
componentLegend.instruction: Instruction - The main task or action requested
componentLegend.example: Examples - One or more input/output examples provided
componentLegend.constraint: Constraints - Rules, limits, or restrictions on the output
componentLegend.format: Output Format - Explicit or strongly implied output structure
componentLegend.audience: Target audience - who the content is for (optional)

presenceValues.partial: Ambiguous - accepts either detected or not

prompts.label: Vague presentation request
prompts.text: hi so i was thinking, my company is growing and my boss said i need to do a presentation but i've never done one properly, can you help me? maybe like 10 slides? i don't know what you think?

prompts.label: Story request with minimal structure
prompts.text: My daughter loves dinosaurs since she watched a Netflix documentary, she has ADHD so she needs something dynamic, but anyway can you write a story for her? It can be short.

prompts.label: Contradictory instructions
prompts.text: I want a text that's formal but casual, long but summarized, technical but easy for beginners, about AI, machine learning, ChatGPT, Claude and the future of tech. Not too long. But complete. For my LinkedIn.

prompts.label: Email with context
prompts.text: Help me write an email to a client who went silent. It needs to be polite but make it clear that I'm waiting for a response.

prompts.label: Explanation with audience context
prompts.text: Explain how compound interest works in a simple way. I'm 20 years old and never studied finance.

prompts.label: Workout plan with constraints
prompts.text: Create a workout plan for weight loss. I have access to a gym and can train 4 times a week.

prompts.label: Financial advice with role
prompts.text: You are a financial advisor. A 28-year-old wants to start investing with $500/month. Suggest 3 investment strategies explaining the risk level of each one.

prompts.label: UX microcopy with format
prompts.text: You are a UX writer. Write 5 onboarding microcopy messages for a personal finance app targeting millennials. Each message should be under 15 words, friendly in tone, and displayed on the first-use screen. Format: numbered list.

prompts.label: Rewrite with example pattern
prompts.text: Rewrite the sentence below to sound more confident and direct, without using passive voice. Like this: "Mistakes were made" → "We made mistakes". Now rewrite: "The report was submitted late by the team."

prompts.label: Meal plan with full context
prompts.text: You are a nutritionist. Create a 5-day meal plan for a vegetarian woman, 35 years old, training 3x per week, with lactose intolerance. Each day must include breakfast, lunch, and dinner. No supplements. Format as a table: Day | Meal | Dish | Calories.

prompts.label: Instagram captions with full anatomy
prompts.text: ok so i'm a social media manager for a small coffee shop in Austin, Texas. we have a really loyal local following (like 8k on instagram) but our posts have been feeling kind of bland lately and i want to fix that. our vibe is very "third wave coffee but not pretentious about it" — we care about quality but we also just want people to feel at home. our regulars are mostly remote workers, college students, and young professionals who come in to work or hang out. they hate when brands try too hard. i need 5 instagram captions for photos of our drinks. nothing too salesy, more like something a cool friend would say. definitely no "life is better with coffee" type stuff, that's exactly what i'm trying to get away from. keep it short, ideally under 15 words each, and no hashtags in the caption itself (we add those separately). here's an example of a caption we posted that actually did really well: "made this one a little too pretty to drink. almost." that kind of energy is what i'm going for. can you write 5 captions like that? just list them out, nothing else needed

prompts.label: Email marketing subject lines
prompts.text: You are a senior email marketing copywriter. A SaaS company is launching a new AI-powered project management tool targeting small business owners with 5–20 employees who struggle with team organization. Write 3 subject lines for a promotional email campaign. Like this example — Feature: "Smart Deadlines" → Subject line: "Never miss a deadline again (your team will thank you)". Each subject line must be under 10 words, create urgency, and avoid spam trigger words like "free" or "guaranteed". Format: numbered list with the subject line followed by a one-sentence explanation of why it works.

prompts.label: Onboarding flow for fintech app
prompts.text:
You are an experienced product manager and UX researcher with 10+ years working at B2B SaaS companies.

## Context
A fintech startup is preparing to launch a mobile budgeting app for freelancers in the US market. The target audience is self-employed professionals aged 25–40, with irregular income, who have little financial literacy and no accountant. The app has three core features:
- Automatic income categorization
- Tax reserve calculator (US-based, self-employment tax)
- Monthly spending reports

## Your Task
Create a complete onboarding flow for first-time users of this app. The onboarding must guide the user from account creation to their first dashboard view.

For each onboarding step, follow this structure:
- Step name
- Goal of this step
- Main screen copy (title + subtitle)
- Primary CTA button text
- One microcopy tip shown below the CTA

## Example
Step: "Connect your bank"
Goal: Reduce manual input friction
Title: "See your money in one place"
Subtitle: "We use bank-level encryption. Your credentials are never stored."
CTA: "Connect securely"
Tip: "Takes less than 2 minutes. You can skip this and add manually later."

## Constraints
- Maximum 5 onboarding steps
- All copy must be written at a 6th-grade reading level
- No financial jargon (avoid: "ledger", "reconcile", "liability", "gross income")
- Tone: warm, encouraging, never patronizing
- Each CTA must be action-oriented and start with a verb
- Do not include a paywall or premium upsell during onboarding

## Output Format
Return each step as a clearly labeled block using the structure from the example above. After all steps, add a short section called "Onboarding Rationale" (3–5 sentences) explaining the key UX decisions made.

prompts.label: Daily check-in prompts (mental health)
prompts.text:
You are a senior content strategist with 8+ years of experience in digital health and wellness brands.

## Context
A mental health startup is launching a new feature inside their app called "Daily Check-in" — a 2-minute guided journaling prompt sent every morning via push notification. The feature is brand new and this is the first batch of content being created for it. The app already has 200k active users and an NPS of 72.

## Target Audience
Adults aged 22–38, living in urban areas, who experience mild to moderate anxiety and are already somewhat familiar with therapy or self-help concepts. They are busy, skeptical of overly positive language, and respond better to honesty and validation than to motivational clichés.

## Tone and Style
Warm but grounded. Never toxic-positive. Write like a trusted friend who has done therapy, not like a wellness influencer. Short sentences. No exclamation marks. Avoid words like: "journey", "thrive", "empower", "transform".

## Your Task
Write 5 daily check-in prompts for the app's morning push notification. Each prompt should make the user pause, reflect, and feel seen — not pressured to perform or feel a certain way.

## Example
Good prompt: "What's one thing you're quietly dreading today? Just naming it can help."
Bad prompt: "Start your day with gratitude! What are you thankful for this morning? 🌟"

The good example works because it acknowledges difficulty without forcing positivity. The bad example fails because it uses an exclamation mark, an emoji, and assumes the user is in a good headspace.

## Constraints
- Each prompt must be between 10 and 20 words
- No emojis
- No exclamation marks
- No yes/no questions — all prompts must invite open reflection
- Do not repeat the same opening word across the 5 prompts

## Output Format
Return a numbered list with 5 prompts.

prompts.label: LinkedIn bio (human-style)
prompts.text: hey so i'm trying to write a bio for my linkedin profile. i'm a UX designer with about 4 years of experience, i've worked mostly with fintech and healthtech startups. i want it to sound professional but not robotic, like i'm a real person. please keep it under 150 words and don't start with "I am" because everyone does that. something like how my last bio started with a question and it got a lot of profile views. can you write one for me?

prompts.label: Etsy candle descriptions
prompts.text:
i run a small etsy shop selling handmade candles and i want to write better product descriptions. my candles are soy-based, hand-poured, and i use essential oils only — no synthetic fragrance. my customers are usually women 25–45 who care about clean ingredients and aesthetic packaging.

right now my descriptions are super boring, just like "lavender soy candle, 8oz, burns up to 50 hours." i want something that feels more lifestyle-y and makes people actually want to buy it. but not over the top, i hate when it sounds fake. can you rewrite that description as an example of what i'm going for? keep it under 80 words.

prompts.label: Job interview prep
prompts.text:
can you help me prep for a job interview? i'm interviewing for a senior account executive role at a B2B SaaS company that sells HR software. i have 6 years in sales, mostly in tech, and i'm really good at enterprise deals but i struggle with behavioral questions, they always catch me off guard.

give me 5 practice questions that are likely to come up, and for each one write me an example answer using my background. keep the answers concise, like something i could actually say in 90 seconds. don't make them sound scripted, i want to sound natural.

prompts.label: Nurse newsletter intro
prompts.text:
i'm a nurse and i want to start a newsletter about healthcare from an insider perspective. my audience would be other nurses and maybe nursing students who are burnt out and need someone to just be real with them about the industry.

i want the tone to be honest and a little dark-humored — not unprofessional, but not corporate wellness BS either. think like a venting session with a colleague after a long shift.

can you write the first edition intro paragraph? just one paragraph, maybe 100–120 words. something that makes people feel like "ok this person gets it" right away.

prompts.label: Team deadlines email
prompts.text:
my team keeps missing deadlines and i need to send an email addressing it without sounding like i'm attacking anyone. we're a remote team of 8 people, pretty flat hierarchy, i'm the lead but not technically their manager.

the last time someone brought this up in a meeting it got awkward so i want to do it over email this time. i want it to be direct but not passive aggressive, and i want to end with a clear next step — like scheduling a quick 30 min sync to align on how we handle this going forward.

can you draft that email? subject line included.

prompts.label: Dinner party menu
prompts.text:
i'm learning to cook and i want to make something impressive for a dinner party this weekend. there will be 6 people, one is vegetarian and one is allergic to nuts. i want to do a 3-course meal but i'm not super advanced so nothing too complicated. i have a normal home kitchen, no fancy equipment.

something like a bruschetta to start would be great — that's the kind of vibe i'm going for. can you suggest the full 3-course menu with one option per course? just the dish names and a one-line description of each, i'll ask for the recipes separately.

prompts.label: UX grant summary
prompts.text:
i'm applying for a UX research grant and i need to write a 200-word project summary. the research is about how elderly users (65+) interact with banking apps and where the biggest friction points are. the grant is from a nonprofit focused on digital inclusion.

i want the summary to sound serious and well-researched but still accessible — the reviewers aren't all designers or researchers, some are just nonprofit board members. avoid overly academic language.

here's my rough version that needs to be cleaned up and made more compelling:
"This project will study how old people use banking apps. We will interview users and find problems. Results will help designers make better apps."

can you rewrite it properly? 200 words max, no bullet points.

prompts.label: Cold email for freelance design
prompts.text:
i need help writing a cold email to reach out to potential clients for my freelance graphic design business. i specialize in brand identity for food and beverage brands — logos, packaging, that kind of thing.

i want to reach out to small craft breweries because i think they really need better branding and they're underserved. the email should be short, i know nobody reads long cold emails. maybe 4–5 sentences max. don't make it sound desperate or like a template, it should feel personal even if i'm sending it to multiple people.

something like leading with a genuine compliment about their brand before pitching would be cool.

prompts.label: Compound interest for students
prompts.text:
i'm a high school teacher and i want to explain the concept of compound interest to my 10th grade students in a way that actually makes sense to them. they're 15–16 years old, they don't care about retirement, and they zone out the second i say anything that sounds like school math.

i want to use an example they'd actually relate to — like something with sneakers or a social media account growing. can you write a short explanation i could read out loud in class? keep it under 2 minutes of speaking time, so roughly 250–300 words. no formulas, just the concept in plain english.

prompts.label: First 1:1 meeting questions
prompts.text:
i just got promoted to manage a team for the first time and i want to set the right tone in my first 1:1 meetings with each person. i have 5 direct reports, all more experienced than me, and i'm a little nervous they won't take me seriously.

i don't want to come in too strong or make it about me — i want it to feel like i'm genuinely trying to understand them and what they need. i've seen managers do the whole "here are my expectations" thing in the first meeting and it always feels tone-deaf.

can you give me a list of 8 questions i should ask in that first 1:1? mix of professional and a bit more personal, nothing too invasive. just the questions, no explanations needed.

prompts.label: Meeting reschedule message
prompts.text: I need to write a message to my team letting them know that our Friday meeting is being moved to Monday at 10am. We've been having these weekly syncs for a while and I want to make sure everyone shows up. Can you help me write something professional but not too formal? We're a pretty casual team.

prompts.label: Protein bar product descriptions
prompts.text: write product descriptions for our new line of protein bars. our customers are fitness-focused adults. don't make it sound like every other supplement brand — we hate phrases like "fuel your gains" or "crush your goals". also no all-caps words, no exclamation marks, and nothing that sounds like it was written by a gym bro. the descriptions should go on our shopify product pages, so keep each one between 60 and 80 words.

prompts.label: Vague workout plan
prompts.text: Come up with a workout routine.

prompts.label: Basic pet advice
prompts.text: Tell me about caring for a dog

prompts.label: Simple resume edit
prompts.text: I just graduated college, can you help edit my resume for job applications?

prompts.label: Travel itinerary ideas
prompts.text: Planning a trip to Europe next summer, suggest some itineraries.

prompts.label: Product description help
prompts.text: I sell handmade jewelry online, write descriptions for a few pieces.

prompts.label: Lesson plan outline
prompts.text: Teaching math to kids, need a basic lesson plan for fractions.

prompts.label: Casual book summary
prompts.text: Hey, I've got this book on history that I'm supposed to summarize for my class, but it's super long. Can you give me a quick rundown, maybe like 500 words, keeping it engaging?

prompts.label: Informal marketing slogans
prompts.text: Running a coffee shop, want some catchy slogans for our new menu. Something fun and upbeat, list out 10 or so.

prompts.label: Conversational poem request
prompts.text: I need a poem about nature for my friend's birthday card. Make it short, no more than 12 lines, and kinda whimsical if possible.

prompts.label: Relaxed data analysis
prompts.text: Got this dataset from work, it's sales numbers over the year. Help me analyze trends, perhaps in a bullet point list, nothing too fancy.

prompts.label: Casual translation task
prompts.text: Translate this paragraph from English to Spanish for my blog. Here's an example sentence: 'The quick brown fox' → 'El zorro marrón rápido'. Keep it natural-sounding.

prompts.label: Structured joke generation
prompts.text: Act as a comedian. Generate 5 jokes about technology. Each under 50 words, no offensive content. Present them in a numbered list.

prompts.label: Organized financial tips
prompts.text: You are a financial advisor. In the context of rising inflation, provide tips for budgeting. Include examples like 'Track expenses daily → Save 10%'. Limit to 8 tips. Format as a table.

prompts.label: Detailed home decor
prompts.text: Act as an interior designer. For a small apartment, suggest decor ideas. Keep suggestions practical and affordable. Output in bullet points with headings.

prompts.label: Comprehensive gardening guide
prompts.text: You are an expert gardener. I'm new to gardening in a urban setting with limited space. Create a beginner's guide to growing herbs. For example: Basil needs full sun → Plant in south-facing window. No more than 1000 words, avoid technical jargon. Format as sections with headings. Targeting novice city dwellers. Make it encouraging and optimistic.

prompts.label: Basic blog topic
prompts.text: write a blog about dogs.

prompts.label: Vague brainstorming request
prompts.text: give me ideas for a video

prompts.label: Local bakery flyer
prompts.text: I'm starting a small bakery in my town and need a flyer. Write the text for it.

prompts.label: Homework translation task
prompts.text: Can you help me translate this paragraph into French? It's for my homework.

prompts.label: Japan trip packing
prompts.text: What should I pack for a trip to Japan in November? I'm going for two weeks.

prompts.label: Black hole article summary
prompts.text: Summarize this article about black holes. I don't want to read the whole thing.

prompts.label: Late order client apology
prompts.text: Hey, I need to write an apology message to a client because we shipped their order late. Can you write a draft? Make it sound sincere but professional. Keep it short, under 100 words.

prompts.label: Plumber SEO keywords
prompts.text: I'm planning a local SEO strategy for a plumber in Chicago. What are some good keywords they should target? I need the output formatted as a table. The audience is local homeowners.

prompts.label: Python scraper 403 bypass
prompts.text: You are a Python expert. I'm trying to write a script that scrapes a website, but I keep getting a 403 error. How do I bypass it? Show me the code with comments.

prompts.label: Vintage car podcast intro
prompts.text: Write a short intro for my new podcast about vintage cars. Target audience is millennial car enthusiasts. Give me a punchy tone. No cheesy sound effects mentioned.

prompts.label: Fantasy elf backstory
prompts.text: I need a character backstory for a fantasy novel. She is an elf who hates magic. Give me three paragraphs. Example of her vibe: 'I'd rather use a rusty dagger than cast a spell.'

prompts.label: Real estate home listing
prompts.text: Act as a real estate agent. I am selling my 3-bedroom house with a big backyard. Write a listing description. It must be under 150 words. Format it as a bulleted list of features. Make the tone inviting and warm.

prompts.label: Student budgeting guide
prompts.text: You are a financial advisor. Write a budgeting guide for college students. My audience is freshmen who have never managed money before. Use a conversational tone. Do not include complex jargon like 'amortization'. Structure it with clear subheadings.

prompts.label: Eco-friendly bottle description
prompts.text: Act as an expert copywriter. Write a product description for an eco-friendly water bottle. Context: the bottle is made of recycled ocean plastic. Format as a list of benefits. Constraint: exactly 3 sentences. Example of style: 'Drink sustainably. Live boldly.' Tone should be energetic.

prompts.label: Best man wedding toast
prompts.text: Act as a senior speechwriter. Context: My brother is getting married and I am the best man, but I am terrible at public speaking. Write a wedding toast for me. The audience will be his conservative family and our wild college friends. Tone: sentimental but funny. Constraint: Do not swear. Example of a joke to include: 'He's the only guy I know who can get lost in his own driveway.' Format the output as a script with pause cues in brackets.

prompts.label: RH - Política de férias
prompts.text: Cria uma política de férias pra empresa.

prompts.label: Financeiro - Explicação DRE
prompts.text: Me explica DRE.

prompts.label: Marketing - Post Instagram
prompts.text: Faz um post pro Instagram.

prompts.label: Jurídico - Contrato
prompts.text: Preciso de um contrato.

prompts.label: TI - Erro no sistema
prompts.text: Tá dando erro no sistema, o que pode ser?

prompts.label: RH - Treinamento D&I
prompts.text: Quero criar um treinamento sobre diversidade e inclusão pra equipe. Me ajuda?

prompts.label: Financeiro - Planilha gastos
prompts.text: Me faz uma planilha de controle de gastos. É pra uso pessoal mesmo, tô tentando organizar minhas finanças.

prompts.label: Vendas - E-mail follow-up
prompts.text: Escreve um e-mail de follow-up pra um cliente que sumiu depois da proposta.

prompts.label: Comunicação - Home office
prompts.text: Preciso avisar o time que a sexta-feira vai ser home office. Redige o comunicado.

prompts.label: TI - LGPD simples
prompts.text: Explica o que é LGPD de um jeito simples. Vai ser usado num treinamento interno pra funcionários que não são da área de TI.

prompts.label: RH - Processo seletivo Analista
prompts.text: Você é especialista em RH. Preciso criar um processo seletivo para uma vaga de Analista de Dados Pleno. A empresa é uma startup de fintech com 80 funcionários. Sugere as etapas do processo e o que avaliar em cada uma.

prompts.label: Marketing - Headline academia
prompts.text: Você é redator publicitário. Cria 3 opções de headline para um e-mail marketing de reativação de clientes inativos de uma academia. O tom deve ser motivador, sem ser agressivo. Cada headline deve ter no máximo 10 palavras.

prompts.label: Financeiro - Relatório receita
prompts.text: Você é analista financeiro sênior. Nossa empresa apresentou queda de 12% na receita no Q2 comparado ao Q1. Me ajuda a montar um relatório executivo explicando as causas possíveis e próximos passos. Sem usar termos técnicos demais — o público é o conselho.

prompts.label: Vendas - Script cold call
prompts.text: Você é especialista em vendas B2B. Temos um software de gestão de estoque e estamos abordando empresas de varejo de médio porte. Cria um script de cold call de no máximo 3 minutos. Tom: consultivo, não empurra produto.

prompts.label: Jurídico - Riscos demissão
prompts.text: Você é advogado trabalhista. Temos um funcionário que pediu demissão mas quer receber como se fosse demissão sem justa causa. Resumo a situação pra você: ele alega que foi forçado a pedir demissão por pressão do gestor. Quais são os riscos legais pra empresa? Resposta objetiva, em tópicos.

prompts.label: Comunicação - Reestruturação
prompts.text: Você é especialista em comunicação corporativa. Nosso CEO vai anunciar uma reestruturação organizacional que impacta 3 departamentos — sem demissões, mas com mudanças de cargo e liderança. Redija um comunicado interno para ser enviado por e-mail a todos os colaboradores. Tom: transparente, empático e direto. Limite: 300 palavras. Estrutura: parágrafo de abertura, mudanças específicas, e encerramento motivador.

prompts.label: RH - People Analytics Turnover
prompts.text: Você é especialista em people analytics. Dado que nossa taxa de turnover nos últimos 6 meses foi de 18% (média do setor: 11%), analise as hipóteses mais prováveis de causa e sugira 3 ações com impacto de curto prazo. Não inclua ações que demandam mais de 6 meses para implementar. Formato de resposta: tabela com colunas — Hipótese | Ação Sugerida | Prazo.

prompts.label: TI - Checklist Home Office
prompts.text:
Você é especialista em cibersegurança corporativa. Crie um checklist de boas práticas de segurança para funcionários em regime de home office. Público: colaboradores sem formação técnica. Cada item deve ser uma ação prática e direta, em linguagem simples. Mínimo de 10 itens. Não inclua referências a ferramentas pagas. Formato: lista numerada, com uma frase de explicação para cada item.

Exemplo de item:
1. Use senhas longas e únicas para cada conta — senhas curtas são as primeiras a serem descobertas em ataques.

prompts.label: Marketing - Growth SaaS
prompts.text: Você é um growth marketer com experiência em SaaS B2B. Nosso produto tem uma taxa de conversão de trial para pago de 8% (benchmark do setor é 15–20%). Sugira 5 hipóteses de melhoria com base em boas práticas de onboarding e ativação. Para cada hipótese: explique o raciocínio, indique um experimento para validar e estime o esforço de implementação (baixo/médio/alto). Formato: tabela com colunas — Hipótese | Raciocínio | Experimento | Esforço.

prompts.label: Financeiro - Relatório EBITDA
prompts.text:
Você é controller financeiro. Dado que estamos encerrando o ciclo orçamentário do ano e o EBITDA realizado ficou 9% abaixo do orçado, prepare um relatório de análise de desvio para apresentar ao conselho. Inclua: causas principais, impacto por área e recomendações para o próximo ciclo. Não use siglas sem explicar na primeira ocorrência. Tamanho: até 600 palavras. Formato: seções com título em negrito.

Exemplo de estrutura esperada:
**1. Sumário Executivo**
**2. Causas do Desvio**
**3. Impacto por Área**
**4. Recomendações**

prompts.label: Contraditório - Resumo curto/longo
prompts.text: Escreve um resumo executivo do projeto. Precisa ser bem completo e detalhado, mas curto. Não mais que 2 parágrafos.

prompts.label: Ambíguo - Melhora esse texto
prompts.text:
Melhora esse texto:

"A reunião de ontem foi produtiva e discutimos varios pontos importantes sobre o projeto que está em andamento e precisamos alinhar com o time."

prompts.label: Role implícito - Especialista UX
prompts.text: Como especialista em UX, o que você acha do nosso fluxo de checkout?

prompts.label: Sem instrução - Gerente de Produto
prompts.text: Você é gerente de produto. Nosso app de delivery tem 4,2 estrelas na Play Store com 12 mil avaliações. Os principais comentários negativos mencionam lentidão no carregamento, dificuldade em cancelar pedidos e falta de filtros de busca. Nossa equipe de tech tem capacidade para 2 sprints de melhorias antes do próximo trimestre.

prompts.label: Benchmark Máximo - CS SaaS
prompts.text:
Você é um especialista em customer success com experiência em empresas SaaS B2B. Nossa plataforma de automação de marketing tem um churn mensal de 4,2% nos últimos 3 meses, acima da meta de 2,5%. Os dados mostram que os clientes que cancelam têm em comum: baixo engajamento nas primeiras 2 semanas, não completaram o onboarding e não integraram com nenhuma ferramenta de CRM.

Sua tarefa: proponha um plano de intervenção de 30 dias focado em reduzir o churn para a meta. Inclua no mínimo 3 ações táticas, responsável sugerido por ação (CS, produto ou marketing), e métrica de sucesso para cada ação. Não inclua ações que dependam de mudanças de produto com prazo maior que 1 sprint.

Exemplo de formato esperado:
| Ação | Responsável | Métrica de Sucesso | Prazo |
|------|-------------|-------------------|-------|
| Criar sequência de e-mails de onboarding | Marketing | Taxa de conclusão do onboarding ≥ 60% | Semana 1 |

Responda em português, tom executivo, sem jargão excessivo.

prompts.label: RH - Modelo Híbrido
prompts.text: Você é especialista em cultura organizacional. Nossa empresa está indo para um modelo híbrido após 3 anos 100% remoto. Parte do time é contra a mudança. Sugere uma estratégia de comunicação e engajamento para facilitar a transição. Máximo de 5 ações práticas, com responsável sugerido para cada uma.

prompts.label: T&D - Programa de Liderança
prompts.text:
Você é especialista em treinamento e desenvolvimento corporativo. Nossa empresa de 300 funcionários vai implementar um programa de liderança para 20 gestores de primeira linha (recém-promovidos, sem experiência anterior de gestão). O programa deve durar 3 meses, com encontros quinzenais de 2 horas cada. Orçamento limitado: prefira metodologias que não dependam de consultoria externa.

Crie o cronograma completo do programa com: tema de cada encontro, objetivo de aprendizagem, atividade prática sugerida e material de apoio (pode ser gratuito ou open source). Não inclua conteúdos que exijam mais de 1h de preparação do facilitador por sessão.
